cityDNA.coreFantasy: A elemental folk city where Heavy Industry / Foundry, Arcane / Alchemical Labs, Records / Archives, Fortress / Border Wall, Underworld / Hidden Economy, and Elemental Crucible Ring of Four Living Fires turn dry stone paving, civic terraces, caldera, dry ground and raw crystal, basalt, copper, arcane glow, quartzite into a distinct civic machine.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: The Crucible should feel like a city that has learned to govern catastrophe through craft, oath, and heat.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: The Crucible organizes public life around controlled transformation: its districts convert Heavy Industry / Foundry, Arcane / Alchemical Labs, Records / Archives, Fortress / Border Wall, Underworld / Hidden Economy into a readable system of authority, labor, memory, and movement.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: The Crucible should be recognized by Raw Crystal, Basalt, Copper, Arcane Glow, Quartzite, Dry Stone Paving, Civic Terraces, Caldera, Dry Ground, Ward Networks, Ritual Geometry, Elemental Convergence, Cipher Glamours, Yellow Intent Glimmers, and the named anchors Elemental Crucible Ring of Four Living Fires, Great Forge Beacon, and Eclipse Rings Sovereign Registry Court.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: The city belongs to elemental folk; its civic habits should grow from the sidecar-backed district spine rather than generic fantasy urbanism.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: Elemental Crucible Ring of Four Living Fires, Great Forge Beacon, Eclipse Rings Sovereign Registry Court, and the five section landmark families symbolize controlled transformation while warning against power that escapes its ritual container.

canonLocks: The Crucible is a elemental folk city, not a generic settlement with cosmetic motifs.
The Wonder is Elemental Crucible Ring of Four Living Fires.
The Beacon is Great Forge Beacon.
The Seat of Power is Eclipse Rings Sovereign Registry Court, located at the District 13 authority center.
The five district sections are Heavy Industry / Foundry, Arcane / Alchemical Labs, Records / Archives, Fortress / Border Wall, Underworld / Hidden Economy.
The board should be treated as twenty-five district slots grouped into five section identities.
Section 1 (Heavy Industry / Foundry) preserves these exact anchor names: Heavy Industry / Foundry Civic Hall, Great Forge Beacon, Smoke-Stack Monument, Crucible Hall Nook.
Section 2 (Arcane / Alchemical Labs) preserves these exact anchor names: Arcane / Alchemical Labs Civic Hall, Alchemy Lab, Blue-Lamp Monument, Formula Archive Nook.
Section 3 (Records / Archives) preserves these exact anchor names: Records / Archives Hall, Archive Tower, Records Archives Gold-Seal Monument, Copyist Hall Nook.
Section 4 (Fortress / Border Wall) preserves these exact anchor names: Fortress / Border Wall Civic Hall, Border Wall, Woven Banners Monument, Watch Bastion Nook.
Section 5 (Underworld / Hidden Economy) preserves these exact anchor names: Underworld / Hidden Economy Civic Hall, Black Market Arcade, Mist Veils Monument, Understreet Vault Nook.
provisionalCanon: Individual district-slot names beyond exact sidecar anchors are organizational labels for the 25-seat board, not new landmark canon.
Minor street customs should be derived from the section spine unless future source files provide more exact anchors.
rejectedReads: The Crucible as a generic fantasy city without its named Wonder, Beacon, and Seat of Power.
Replacing exact sidecar anchor names with generic labels such as market, temple, tower, wall, or archive.
Treating district section labels as decorative instead of civic systems that drive scenes and assets.
cautionFlags: Preserve exact named anchors from sidecars.
Use the culture identity respectfully as civic design language rather than costume.
Keep all 25 district slots legible even when five slots share one section identity.
Do not add unsupported individual district landmark names beyond the source-backed anchors.

environment.environmentalLogic: The Crucible's environment should make Heavy Industry / Foundry, Arcane / Alchemical Labs, Records / Archives, Fortress / Border Wall, Underworld / Hidden Economy feel physically necessary. Terrain, light, civic routes, and landmark placement must support movement through all five section identities rather than read as interchangeable scenery.

skyline: Elemental Crucible Ring of Four Living Fires is the wonder-scale skyline identifier.
Eclipse Rings Sovereign Registry Court marks the central authority mass at District 13.
Great Forge Beacon must remain visible as the public signal landmark.
Section landmarks should form a readable ring of Heavy Industry / Foundry, Arcane / Alchemical Labs, Records / Archives, Fortress / Border Wall, Underworld / Hidden Economy.

civilization.people: The Crucible's people are elemental folk; they should be written through shared civic habits, public spaces, work rhythms, and the visible demands of the five section identities.
socialClasses: officials and stewards tied to Eclipse Rings Sovereign Registry Court
landmark keepers and civic hall administrators
street vendors, porters, runners, and maintenance crews
families living around the section-specific local anchors
civilization.governance: Authority centers on Eclipse Rings Sovereign Registry Court and is made visible through section civic halls, named landmarks, permissions, public signals, and the citywide response to Great Forge Beacon.
civilization.religion: Religious or ceremonial life should be shown through source-backed shrines, temples, wonders, oath spaces, ancestor sites, or ritual geometry when those appear in the district spine.
customs: citizens orient themselves by the section identity of a street before they identify an address
public notices and signals are interpreted through the Beacon and civic halls
named landmarks act as meeting points, proof points, and local memory anchors
section-specific work routines shape food, clothing, posture, and street traffic
District 13 authority matters even when scenes occur in outer district slots
bodyLanguage: citizens check landmarks and signals before crossing into another section
officials use the posture and tools of their section role
workers treat section anchors as practical wayfinding points
crowds become more formal near the Seat of Power and more local near minor landmarks
speechPatterns: directions use exact landmark names rather than generic descriptions
official speech references permits, seals, routes, oaths, or signals as appropriate to the city
working speech is concrete, location-heavy, and tied to civic function
locals distinguish between the Wonder, Beacon, Seat, and section landmarks without confusion

streets.appearance: Street scenes should make The Crucible immediately identifiable through Raw Crystal, Basalt, Copper, Arcane Glow, Quartzite, Dry Stone Paving, Civic Terraces, Caldera, Dry Ground, Ward Networks, Ritual Geometry, Elemental Convergence, Cipher Glamours, Yellow Intent Glimmers, and the exact section anchors rather than generic fantasy facades.
streets.movement: Movement flows through five section identities and twenty-five district slots. People navigate by Great Forge Beacon, Eclipse Rings Sovereign Registry Court, section civic halls, major landmarks, monuments, and minor nooks.
streets.lighting: Lighting should use Civic Blue Glow, Forge Smoke Columns, Lantern Lit, Mist Veils, White Limestone Civic Blocks, symbolic civic object, with the Beacon and Seat carrying distinct civic emphasis.
streets.weatherEffects: Weather should interact with dry stone paving, civic terraces, caldera, dry ground and the city's source-backed materials, altering visibility, travel speed, public gathering, and signal readability.
streets.dayCycle: Daytime emphasizes section labor: Heavy Industry / Foundry, Arcane / Alchemical Labs, Records / Archives, Fortress / Border Wall, Underworld / Hidden Economy. Civic halls, shops, and public works create the main public rhythm.
streets.nightCycle: Night scenes should preserve the city's identity through landmark silhouettes, beacon light, watch routes, late shops, ritual spaces, and section-specific sounds.

prestigious: workshops or offices near Eclipse Rings Sovereign Registry Court
specialist houses serving Elemental Crucible Ring of Four Living Fires
master artisans tied to section landmarks

housing.wealthy: High-status housing clusters near Eclipse Rings Sovereign Registry Court, major terraces, and prestigious landmark approaches; it should display the city materials cleanly and control sightlines toward Wonder or Beacon.
housing.middle: Middle housing occupies upper rooms, side courts, work-family compounds, and mixed-use streets close to civic halls and section services.
housing.poor: Poorer housing should be practical and specific to local labor, with patched materials, shared courtyards, crowded lanes, and strong dependence on nearby section anchors.

wonder.name: Elemental Crucible Ring of Four Living Fires
wonder.symbolicRole: The citywide symbol of controlled transformation made monumental.
wonder.visualRole: A wonder-scale skyline anchor using Raw Crystal, Basalt, Copper, Arcane Glow, Quartzite, Dry Stone Paving, Civic Terraces, Caldera, Dry Ground, and the elemental folk visual language.
wonder.civicRole: Represents the whole city above the five sections: Heavy Industry / Foundry, Arcane / Alchemical Labs, Records / Archives, Fortress / Border Wall, Underworld / Hidden Economy.
storyHooks: access to Elemental Crucible Ring of Four Living Fires changes public legitimacy
a citywide ritual reveals hidden pressure
the Wonder becomes a navigation or warning reference

beacon.name: Great Forge Beacon
beacon.symbolicRole: Public signal and shared alarm for The Crucible.
beacon.visualRole: A section-scale signal landmark that must read immediately as the city Beacon.
beacon.civicRole: Coordinates public response, civic warnings, and Conjunction-state attention.
storyHooks: someone controls the first interpretation of a signal
the Beacon redirects traffic between sections
a warning exposes which citizens trust civic authority

seatOfPower.name: Eclipse Rings Sovereign Registry Court
seatOfPower.symbolicRole: Central authority made visible and source-backed.
seatOfPower.visualRole: The District 13 authority complex with formal approaches and city-specific materials.
seatOfPower.civicRole: Seat of government, petitions, records, rulings, and command visibility.
storyHooks: a ruling contradicts a section custom
a sealed record changes public interpretation

districtDioramaGuidance: Create twenty-five district dioramas grouped into five recognizable sections: Heavy Industry / Foundry, Arcane / Alchemical Labs, Records / Archives, Fortress / Border Wall, Underworld / Hidden Economy.
Each district should use its districtNumber and sectionNumber, but avoid baked text labels in imagery.
D13 must read as the Seat of Power district centered on Eclipse Rings Sovereign Registry Court.
Repeated section anchors may appear across five related slots, but each slot needs its own street function, crowd density, and prop emphasis.
Use exact source-backed anchor names for file naming, prompt references, and canon cross-checking.
S1 must preserve Heavy Industry / Foundry with anchors: Heavy Industry / Foundry Civic Hall, Great Forge Beacon, Smoke-Stack Monument, Crucible Hall Nook.
S2 must preserve Arcane / Alchemical Labs with anchors: Arcane / Alchemical Labs Civic Hall, Alchemy Lab, Blue-Lamp Monument, Formula Archive Nook.
S3 must preserve Records / Archives with anchors: Records / Archives Hall, Archive Tower, Records Archives Gold-Seal Monument, Copyist Hall Nook.
S4 must preserve Fortress / Border Wall with anchors: Fortress / Border Wall Civic Hall, Border Wall, Woven Banners Monument, Watch Bastion Nook.
S5 must preserve Underworld / Hidden Economy with anchors: Underworld / Hidden Economy Civic Hall, Black Market Arcade, Mist Veils Monument, Understreet Vault Nook.
mapBackgroundGuidance: Map backgrounds should keep Elemental Crucible Ring of Four Living Fires as the skyline symbol, Eclipse Rings Sovereign Registry Court at the central civic authority, and Great Forge Beacon as the readable public signal.

Arrange the five section identities so The Crucible does not converge with other cities: Heavy Industry / Foundry, Arcane / Alchemical Labs, Records / Archives, Fortress / Border Wall, Underworld / Hidden Economy.

Use terrain cues (Dry Stone Paving, Civic Terraces, Caldera, Dry Ground), material cues (Raw Crystal, Basalt, Copper, Arcane Glow, Quartzite), and magic cues (Ward Networks, Ritual Geometry, Elemental Convergence, Cipher Glamours, Yellow Intent Glimmers) consistently across district clusters.
Show roads, terraces, gates, courts, or paths that make the twenty-five-district tactical layout plausible.
requiredVisualElements: Elemental Crucible Ring of Four Living Fires
elementsToAvoid: extra top-level schema sections or embedded generation notes
generic fantasy city assets that ignore exact anchor names
unlabeled invented landmarks replacing sidecar names
flattening five sections into one visual district
treating culture identity as costume rather than civic design
overcrowded detail that hides Wonder, Beacon, or Seat of Power
